[12:52, 06/02/2026] BHAVYA: Build a small functional Hotel PMS Room Management Dashboard.
This is a functional demo, not a full production system.

IMPORTANT:
* Follow PMS industry standards (Opera / ASI style)
* Keep UI clean, professional, neutral
* Do NOT use flashy colors or decorative design
* Choose layout, spacing, and colors wisely and minimally
* Functionality is more important than visuals

----------------------------------
CORE MODULES
----------------------------------

1. DASHBOARD (Main Screen)

* Top horizontal bar:
  - Menu items: File | Edit | Exit
  - Floor selector buttons: 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 | 6
  - Clicking a floor filters rooms for that floor only

* Left sidebar:
  - Dashboard
  - Room Settings (Admin only)
  - Date selector (common for all users)

* Main area:
  - Show rooms in a grid layout
  - Each room box shows:
    - Room Number
    - Room Type (DD, NK, etc.)
    - Small icons if applicable:
        • Smoking
        • Handicap
  - Non-room must NOT be clickable

* Hover / click on a room:
  - Show popup with:
    - Room No
    - Room Type
    - Dummy Guest Name
    - Dummy Check-in & Check-out date
    - Dummy charges
  - This popup is for demo purpose only

----------------------------------
2. ROOM SETTINGS (ADMIN ONLY)
----------------------------------

This section is only accessible to Admin users.

A) FLOOR SETUP
* Small popup window
* Text box to enter floor number or name
* ADD button
* Added floors appear in list

B) ROOM TYPE SETUP
* Text box for room type name (e.g. DD, NK, Suite)
* Text box for default price (currency format)
* ADD button
* Saved room types appear in dropdowns

C) ROOM CREATION
* Inputs:
    - Room Number
    - Floor (dropdown)
    - Room Type (dropdown from saved room types)
* Checkboxes:
    - Smoking
    - Handicap
    - Non-Room

LOGIC RULES:
* If Non-Room is selected:
    - Room is saved
    - Room is NOT clickable on dashboard
    - Smoking and Handicap icons must NOT appear
* Smoking icon shows only if Smoking is selected
* Handicap icon shows only if Handicap is selected

* ADD button creates the room and shows it on dashboard

----------------------------------
3. DATA STRUCTURE (SIMPLE)
----------------------------------

Use simple internal data storage.

Tables / collections:
* Floors: id, floor_no
* RoomTypes: id, name, price
* Rooms:
   - room_no
   - floor_id
   - room_type
   - smoking (true/false)
   - handicap (true/false)
   - non_room (true/false)

----------------------------------
DESIGN GUIDELINES
----------------------------------

* Do NOT ask me for color preferences
* Choose neutral, professional PMS-style colors
* Clean layout, proper spacing
* Simple icons only
* No animations unless necessary
* Dashboard must look enterprise, not startup-fancy

----------------------------------
GOAL
----------------------------------

Create a small working PMS demo that:
* Clearly explains room logic
* Can be shown to developers or investors
* Demonstrates admin vs user behavior
* Focuses on correctness, not decoration
[12:54, 06/02/2026] BHAVYA: ----------------------------------
MVP QUALITY & VISUAL EXPECTATIONS
----------------------------------

This project must look like a premium hotel PMS MVP.

DESIGN GOALS:
* Enterprise-grade, hotel-industry look
* Clean, calm, professional
* Visually attractive but NOT flashy
* Suitable for hotels, not consumer apps

COLOR & UI GUIDANCE:
* Choose a balanced, professional color palette
* Prefer calm neutral base colors with subtle accents
* Avoid bright neon, gradients, or playful tones
* Use color meaningfully:
    - Room states
    - Active floor selection
    - Hover / focus states

TYPOGRAPHY:
* Use clear, readable fonts
* Prioritize clarity over style
* Proper hierarchy:
    - Titles
    - Labels
    - Room numbers

ROOM VISUAL DESIGN:
* Room boxes should:
    - Look like solid cards
    - Have subtle borders or shadows
    - Clearly display room number and type
* Icons should be minimal and universally understandable
* Non-room must appear visually disabled or muted

INTERACTION QUALITY:
* Hover effects should feel smooth and professional
* Active selections must be visually clear
* Click feedback must be immediate and obvious
* No unnecessary animations

LAYOUT QUALITY:
* Proper spacing and alignment
* Consistent margins
* Grid must feel structured and balanced
* Dashboard should not feel crowded

OVERALL MVP STANDARD:
* This should feel like a real hotel system demo
* Something that can be confidently shown to:
    - Hotel owners
    - Senior developers
    - Investors

IMPORTANT:
* Improve visuals ONLY if they do not change logic
* Never remove or alter functional rules
* Design decisions must support usability and clarity

Each room card must show availability status using ONLY a single vertical border on the RIGHT side.

• Use ONLY a right-side border (no full background color).
• Border thickness: subtle but visible (approx 3–4px).
• No icons, no animations.

Color mapping:
• Green border → Available room
• Red border → Occupied room
• Yellow border → Reserved / Cleaning / Hold

Do NOT apply color to the entire card.
Do NOT use multiple borders.
This border is the only status indicator.

This is required for a professional hotel PMS UI.

**Visualizing Status Updates**

I'm focusing on incorporating a clear visual cue for room status within the Hotel PMS Dashboard's room cards. The approach will involve a vertical border on the right-hand side of each card, with a thickness of 3-4 pixels. The plan is for the border alone to signify the status, without any background color, icons, or animations. The goal is a subtle, yet effective, indicator.


**Refining Status Indicators**

I'm now zeroing in on the data model. I will add a `status` field to the `Room` interface in `types.ts`, which requires a new type for status: 'Available' | 'Occupied' | 'Reserved' | 'Cleaning' | 'Hold'. I must make updates to `constants.tsx` and `components/RoomCard.tsx`. Next will be status selection UI in `components/RoomSettings.tsx`. Simplifying the status options to three based on color is an option.

SYSTEM ROLE:
You are building a professional Hotel PMS (Property Management System).
This is enterprise software used daily by hotel staff.
Clarity, data safety, and mistake-prevention are higher priority than visual creativity.

--------------------------------------------------
GLOBAL PRINCIPLES (MANDATORY)
--------------------------------------------------

• Never guess user intent.
• Never duplicate saved data.
• Never allow accidental overwrites.
• Always prevent invalid or redundant actions.
• If something is already saved, it must not be selectable again.

--------------------------------------------------
PROPERTY MANAGEMENT STRUCTURE
--------------------------------------------------

Property Management consists of THREE independent admin modules:

1. Floor Setup
2. Room Categories (Room Types)
3. Unit Inventory (Rooms)

Each module:
• Opens as a separate page
• Has its own Add / List / Edit / Delete logic
• Does NOT share UI with other modules

--------------------------------------------------
CRITICAL RULE: SELECTION & DUPLICATION PREVENTION
--------------------------------------------------

If an item is already saved in the system:

• It must NOT be selectable again in dropdowns.
• It must NOT appear as an available option.
• It must be disabled or hidden from selection.

Examples:
• A floor already created → cannot be created again.
• A room number already saved → cannot be reused.
• A room category already defined → cannot be added again.

This rule is mandatory across ALL modules.

--------------------------------------------------
MODULE 1: FLOOR SETUP
--------------------------------------------------

Purpose:
Manage hotel floors.

Behavior:
• Admin can add a floor only once.
• If Floor 1 exists, Floor 1 must not be selectable or addable again.
• Floor selection dropdown must exclude already-saved floors.

List View:
Each saved floor must show:
• Floor Name / Number
• Edit icon (✏️) on the LEFT
• Delete icon (🗑️) on the RIGHT

Icons only. No text buttons.

Delete rules:
• Confirm before delete.
• If floor has rooms assigned → prevent deletion with clear message.

--------------------------------------------------
MODULE 2: ROOM CATEGORIES (ROOM TYPES)
--------------------------------------------------

Purpose:
Define room categories (e.g., Standard, Deluxe, Suite).

Behavior:
• Room category name must be unique.
• Once saved, it must not appear again in add/select lists.
• Prevent duplicate category names.

List View:
Each room category must show:
• Category name
• Edit icon (✏️) on the LEFT
• Delete icon (🗑️) on the RIGHT

Icons only. No text labels.

Delete rules:
• If category is used by any room → block deletion.

--------------------------------------------------
MODULE 3: UNIT INVENTORY (ROOMS)
--------------------------------------------------

Purpose:
Create and manage individual rooms.

Behavior:
• Room number must be unique.
• Once saved, the same room number cannot be added again.
• Floor dropdown must show ONLY existing floors.
• Room category dropdown must show ONLY existing categories.
• Already-used combinations must not be selectable again.

Attributes:
• Smoking
• Handicap
• Non-Room (Service / Utility)

If marked as Non-Room:
• Disable guest-related behavior.
• Do not show availability states.

List View:
Each room must show:
• Room Number
• Floor
• Category
• Edit icon (✏️) on the LEFT
• Delete icon (🗑️) on the RIGHT

Icons only. No text buttons.

--------------------------------------------------
EDIT & DELETE ICON RULES (STRICT)
--------------------------------------------------

• Edit icon (✏️):
  - Positioned on the LEFT side of each row/card
  - Opens edit mode with pre-filled data

• Delete icon (🗑️):
  - Positioned on the RIGHT side
  - Requires confirmation
  - Must respect dependency rules

No text buttons like "Edit" or "Delete".
Icons only.

--------------------------------------------------
ERROR PREVENTION & UX SAFETY
--------------------------------------------------

• If user tries to add something that already exists:
  → Block action
  → Show clear message
  → Do not save duplicate

• Disable invalid actions instead of allowing and failing later.

--------------------------------------------------
FINAL CONSTRAINT
--------------------------------------------------

Do not combine modules.
Do not simplify logic.
Do not remove safeguards.
This PMS must behave like real hotel software, not a demo app.

**Analyzing Hotel PMS Logic**

I'm currently dissecting the Imperion Hotel PMS. I've broken it down into three core modules: Floor Setup, Room Categories, and Unit Inventory. I'm focusing on the stringent logic within each. I have overhauled the configuration modules to align with the strict enterprise PMS standards you specified. 

### Key Enhancements:
- **Modular Independence**: Each management section (Floors, Categories, Inventory) is now a standalone page with its own specialized logic.
- **Icon-Only Actions**: Following the Opera/ASI standard, action buttons for "Edit" are strictly on the **LEFT** and "Delete" on the **RIGHT** of all lists, using only professional minimal icons.
- **Duplication Prevention**: Robust validation prevents the creation of duplicate floors, room numbers, or category codes, providing clear error feedback to the user.
- **Dependency Safeguards**: State logic (integrated via the existing `App.tsx` callbacks) prevents the deletion of floors or room categories that have dependent inventory, ensuring system integrity.
- **Professional ASI UI**: Re-styled with a high-contrast, clean enterprise aesthetic using neutral tones, heavy typography for data points, and status-driven visual feedback.
- **Functional Forms**: Form states are now fully interactive, supporting both "Create" and "Update" flows with pre-filled values and clear cancellation paths.

--------------------------------------------------
MANDATORY PRE-ADD VALIDATION (NO EXCEPTIONS)
--------------------------------------------------

The system must NEVER allow an "Add" action
unless all required selections are completed.

The "Add" button must perform validation first.
No silent failure. No partial save.

--------------------------------------------------
MODULE-WISE VALIDATION MESSAGES
--------------------------------------------------

1) FLOOR SETUP

If the user clicks "Add" without selecting or entering a floor:

• Block the action.
• Show a clear, human-readable message:

  "Floor is not chosen yet."

Do NOT:
• Save anything
• Reload the page
• Show technical error text

--------------------------------------------------
2) ROOM CATEGORY (ROOM TYPE) SETUP

If the user clicks "Add" without choosing or entering a room category:

• Block the action.
• Show message:

  "Room type is not chosen yet."

Do NOT:
• Assume a default
• Auto-select a value
• Create empty records

--------------------------------------------------

If the user clicks "Add Room" without required selections:

Validation must be checked in this order:

• Room number missing →
  "Room number is not chosen yet."

• Floor not selected →
  "Floor is not chosen yet."

• Room type not selected →
  "Room type is not chosen yet."

Only show ONE message at a time
based on the first missing requirement.

--------------------------------------------------
VALIDATION UX RULES
--------------------------------------------------

• Messages must be:
  - Simple
  - Clear
  - Non-technical
  - Calm (not alarming)

• Do NOT use:
  - Error codes
  - System jargon
  - Red alert dialogs

• Prefer:
  - Inline message near the button OR
  - Soft notification/snackbar

--------------------------------------------------
ABSOLUTE RULE
--------------------------------------------------

Never create:
• Empty records
• Partial records
• Placeholder records

If validation fails → nothing is saved.

This validation logic is mandatory across all admin modules.

**Initiating Validation Logic**

  id="floorNumber"
  name="floorNumber"
  type="text"
  inputmode="numeric"
  autocomplete="off"
  placeholder=""
  onkeydown="return allowOnlyNumbers(event)"
  onpaste="return false"
  oninput="sanitizeFloorNumber(this)"
/>

<script>
function allowOnlyNumbers(e) {
  const allowedKeys = [
    'Backspace', 'Delete', 'ArrowLeft', 'ArrowRight', 'Tab'
  ];
  if (allowedKeys.includes(e.key)) return true;
  if (/^[0-9]$/.test(e.key)) return true;
  return false; // BLOCK EVERYTHING ELSE

function sanitizeFloorNumber(input) {
  input.value = input.value.replace(/[^0-9]/g, '');
